title: Add local username/password auth gate, private encrypted chat, mood/burnout detection, and therapist prompt flow

requirements.text: Add a custom local username/password authentication system that must be completed before a user can access the Anonymous Chat experience.

quotes: now add the local user login before the chat feature
a custom username password system which includes high security detail

acceptanceCriteria: When the user clicks the existing CTA that currently opens the experience, they are routed to a local sign-in/sign-up flow instead of the chat UI unless already authenticated
After successful local login, the user can access the experience view (chat + data entry) without errors
Logout is available and returns the user to the pre-login state, preventing access to chat until login again

requirements.text: Implement secure credential handling for local auth: secure storage, robust transmission, strict user policies including password complexity AND password rotation enforced simultaneously.

quotes: includes high security detail , as in secure storage,  robust transmission, strict user policy

acceptanceCriteria: Signup rejects passwords that do not meet a strict complexity policy (define and display requirements in the UI)
Login/signup flows enforce a password rotation policy (define and display the rotation window and required behavior)
Passwords are never stored or returned in plaintext by the backend; only non-sensitive account metadata may be returned
Frontend does not log passwords or include them in query keys / URLs / local storage

requirements.text: Add a simple local CAPTCHA-style challenge as an additional authentication layer during account creation and/or sign-in, implemented without third-party services.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth screens include a local challenge step that must be passed before submitting credentials
The challenge regenerates on demand and after repeated failed attempts
User-facing errors are shown in English and do not expose internal details

requirements.text: Extend the Motoko backend to support local user accounts and sessions suitable for gating access to chat/data-entry APIs, while keeping to the single-actor architecture.

quotes: add the local user login before the chat feature

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes canister methods needed for sign-up, sign-in, sign-out, and checking current auth status
Chat and data-entry write operations are rejected when the caller is not locally authenticated
Existing chat retrieval and data listing continue to work as expected for authenticated users
State is persisted across upgrades using stable storage; if a migration is required, it is implemented using backend/migration.mo per the conditional migration policy

requirements.text: Implement a simplified private encryption layer for anonymous chat such that messages are end-to-end encrypted between clients and also encrypted for storage/transport on the platform (store ciphertext, not plaintext).

quotes: create end to end encryption comms using protocols like open source signal protocol
yes you can use the simplified encryption layer but make it so that it should be private
both, e2ee between clients and storage/transport on the IC

acceptanceCriteria: Frontend encrypts outgoing chat messages before sending them to the backend; backend stores and returns encrypted payloads rather than plaintext
Frontend decrypts messages for display when it has the required key material; if it cannot decrypt, it shows a clear English placeholder state (without leaking ciphertext as normal text)
No third-party encryption services/protocol implementations are introduced; the solution remains a simplified, app-contained approach
The encryption feature does not break the existing polling/refresh behavior of the chat UI (React Query refetch interval)

requirements.text: Add an IC-hosted/on-device rule-based classifier that analyzes submitted chat text to identify mood and mental burnout signals, and generate a deterministic, rule-based supportive response.

quotes: use the consensus AI for the identification of moods and mental burnouts
yes a simple on device/IC-hosted rule-based classifier

acceptanceCriteria: When a user submits a chat message, the app produces a rule-based analysis result (e.g., mood label and burnout risk level) and a supportive response text in English
The classifier is rule-based (no external AI API calls) and runs within platform constraints
The user can see the generated supportive response as part of the chat flow (clearly identified as a system/assistant response)

requirements.text: After the system produces the supportive response to the submitted chat text, present a dedicated screen (or modal step) that offers to connect the user to a licensed therapist and states that the first session will be free if the user proceeds.

quotes: then show a screen we will be connecting you to a licensed therapist if you wish to proceed , the first session will be free

acceptanceCriteria: After the supportive response is shown, the user is prompted with a clear English UI offering to proceed to therapist connection and stating the first session is free
The user can choose to proceed or decline and return to the chat experience without losing the current conversation state
No external therapist scheduling/payment integrations are added; the screen is an in-app prompt/flow only

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; add backend/migration.mo only if needed for stable state upgrades
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only shadcn components) or immutable hook entrypoints listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT
No third-party authentication providers; implement only the requested custom username/password system
No external CAPTCHA services; CAPTCHA must be a local challenge
No external AI/LLM APIs; mood/burnout detection must be rule-based and IC-hosted/on-device
Signal Protocol is not required/assumed; implement a simplified private encryption approach within constraints
nonGoals: Real-time messaging via websockets or push notifications
Integration with external therapist networks, booking systems, email/SMS, or payments
Implementing the full Signal Protocol specification
Adding third-party identity providers beyond the existing Internet Identity hook (do not rely on it for the requested local auth)
